purpose: Hardware monitoring container. Polls and manages fans, PSUs, transceivers, temperatures, and LEDs on the physical platform. Bridges vendor-specific platform APIs (sonic-platform Python plugins) with STATE_DB so that the rest of SONiC can observe hardware health without platform-specific knowledge. Executes thermal control policy (fan speed curves) and raises syslog alerts on hardware faults.

daemons.name: xcvrd
daemons.role: Transceiver daemon. Polls all physical transceiver slots via the platform API to detect insertion/removal events and read EEPROM data (type, vendor, serial, DOM thresholds). Writes transceiver presence, type, and DOM (Digital Optical Monitoring) data to STATE_DB TRANSCEIVER_INFO_TABLE and TRANSCEIVER_DOM_SENSOR_TABLE. Also handles CMIS (Common Management Interface Specification) state machine for 400G+ modules.

daemons.name: thermalctld
daemons.role: Thermal control daemon. Reads all temperature sensors and fan tachometers via the platform API on a polling interval. Executes a thermal control algorithm (vendor-supplied policy or SONiC default) to set fan speeds proportionally to thermal load. Writes current temperature and fan state to STATE_DB. Triggers emergency thermal shutdown if any sensor exceeds its critical threshold.

reads: CONFIG_DB: DEVICE_METADATA (platform name, hwsku for policy selection)

daemons.name: psud
daemons.role: PSU daemon. Polls all PSU slots via the platform API for presence, operational status, input/output voltage, current, and power. Raises syslog CRIT alerts on PSU absence or failure. Writes PSU health data to STATE_DB PSU_INFO_TABLE. Required for 'show platform psu' output.

daemons.name: syseepromd
daemons.role: System EEPROM daemon. Reads the chassis EEPROM once at startup using the platform API (platform-specific I2C/SPI path). Writes EEPROM fields (serial number, hardware revision, manufacture date, MAC base address, etc.) to STATE_DB EEPROM_INFO table and to /proc/sonic-platform/eeprom. Required for 'show platform syseeprom'.

daemons.name: ledd
daemons.role: LED daemon. Controls front-panel port LEDs to reflect link and activity state. Reads port oper_status from STATE_DB and calls the platform LED API to set LED color and blink pattern. Not present on all platforms -- vendor platform plugin must implement the chassis LED API.

writes: Platform hardware LED registers (via platform API)

daemons.name: ledmgrd
daemons.role: LED manager. Complements ledd by managing system-level LEDs (STATUS, MASTER, FAN, PSU) based on overall system health signals. Reads health status from STATE_DB and sets chassis-level LEDs via the platform API. Provides visual indication of system health on the front panel.

writes: Platform hardware system LED registers (via platform API)

daemons.name: pcied
daemons.role: PCIe health daemon. Scans all PCIe devices on the platform against an expected device list (pcieinfo.yaml in the platform directory). Reports missing or unexpected PCIe devices to STATE_DB and syslog. Detects hardware errors on the PCIe bus that may indicate partial platform initialization or hardware failure.

key_data_flows.path: Physical transceiver insertion -> xcvrd platform API poll -> vendor platform plugin reads SFP/QSFP EEPROM (I2C) -> xcvrd writes STATE_DB:TRANSCEIVER_INFO_TABLE + TRANSCEIVER_DOM_SENSOR_TABLE -> 'show interfaces transceiver' reads STATE_DB
key_data_flows.notes: If transceiver shows as absent in STATE_DB, the failure is in xcvrd's EEPROM read or the platform plugin. Check xcvrd log for I2C errors before assuming a bad module.

key_data_flows.path: thermalctld polls platform API (temperature sensors + fan tachometers) -> applies thermal policy algorithm -> calls platform API fan.set_speed() -> writes STATE_DB:FAN_INFO_TABLE + TEMPERATURE_INFO_TABLE -> 'show platform temperature/fan' reads STATE_DB
key_data_flows.notes: If a sensor read fails, thermalctld enters fallback mode and drives fans to 100%. Persistent fallback mode indicates a broken platform API or hardware sensor fault.

key_data_flows.path: psud polls platform API for each PSU slot -> reads presence, status, voltage, current -> writes STATE_DB:PSU_INFO_TABLE -> on fault: raises syslog CRIT alert -> 'show platform psu' reads STATE_DB
key_data_flows.notes: psud does not take corrective action -- it only monitors and reports. Alerting depends on syslog. If PSU failure is not generating alerts, check psud health first.

key_data_flows.flow: System EEPROM read (hardware -> STATE_DB, one-time at startup)
key_data_flows.path: syseepromd starts -> reads chassis EEPROM via platform API (I2C address vendor-specific) -> parses TLV fields -> writes STATE_DB:EEPROM_INFO -> 'show platform syseeprom' reads STATE_DB
key_data_flows.notes: This is a one-shot read at daemon start. If EEPROM data is stale or missing, restart syseepromd -- it will re-read from hardware.

warm_restart.mechanism: pmon daemons are stateless pollers; no ASIC state to reconcile. Container restart causes a brief gap in monitoring but no traffic impact. Transceiver DOM and platform sensor data repopulates within one polling cycle (typically 60 seconds) after restart.
warm_restart.enable_command: N/A -- warm restart not applicable to pmon
warm_restart.caveat: xcvrd restart may briefly clear TRANSCEIVER_INFO_TABLE entries, causing flaps in monitoring systems that watch transceiver presence. Not traffic-impacting but may generate false alarms.

restart_impact: No traffic impact. pmon only reads hardware sensors and writes to STATE_DB. Restarting pmon causes a monitoring gap: fan speeds hold at their last set value (hardware retains the last commanded speed), temperature alerts are suspended, and transceiver DOM data goes stale until daemons repopulate STATE_DB. The thermalctld fan control loop resumes within seconds of restart and re-reads all sensors.

restart_commands.xcvrd_only: docker exec pmon supervisorctl restart xcvrd
restart_commands.thermalctld_only: docker exec pmon supervisorctl restart thermalctld
restart_commands.psud_only: docker exec pmon supervisorctl restart psud

state_verification_commands: show interfaces transceiver eeprom (full EEPROM dump from STATE_DB)
show interfaces transceiver dom (DOM sensor readings from STATE_DB)

common_issues.issue: xcvrd fails to detect transceiver -- module absent in STATE_DB
symptoms: show interfaces transceiver presence shows 'Not present' for a populated port
redis-cli -n 6 hgetall 'TRANSCEIVER_INFO|EthernetX' returns empty
show interfaces status shows port as down despite module inserted
xcvrd log contains 'EEPROM read failed' or 'platform_chassis.get_sfp() raised exception'
causes: Vendor platform plugin not implementing get_sfp() or read_eeprom() correctly
I2C bus error or transceiver EEPROM page read timeout (bad or marginal module)
xcvrd process crashed or stuck in a retry loop on a different port
Module type mismatch -- CMIS module on a port with xcvrd lacking CMIS state machine support
Physical: module not fully seated, dirty connector, or hardware I2C bus fault
diagnosis: docker exec pmon tail -100 /var/log/pmon/xcvrd.log -- look for 'EEPROM read error', 'exception', 'failed to get SFP'
docker exec pmon supervisorctl status xcvrd -- confirm daemon is running, not in FATAL state
show interfaces transceiver presence -- identify which ports are affected (single port vs all ports)
If single port: reseat the transceiver module. Try the module in a different port to isolate hardware vs software
If all ports: platform plugin likely broken -- check 'show platform summary' for platform detection
docker exec pmon python3 -c "import sonic_platform; p = sonic_platform.platform.Platform(); c = p.get_chassis(); print(c.get_sfp(0).get_presence())" -- test platform API directly
journalctl -u pmon --since '10 minutes ago' | grep xcvrd -- broader syslog view
resolution: Single bad module: replace transceiver; re-run 'show interfaces transceiver presence' to confirm
Platform plugin exception: update sonic-platform-modules package for the platform (vendor BSP update)
I2C bus error: 'docker exec pmon supervisorctl restart xcvrd' to clear xcvrd state and retry EEPROM reads
CMIS module: verify SONiC version supports CMIS for this platform; check vendor release notes
If xcvrd in crash loop: 'sudo systemctl restart pmon' -- full container restart resets all platform API state

common_issues.issue: thermalctld triggers thermal shutdown -- system powers off unexpectedly
symptoms: System powers off without warning or with very short notice in syslog
syslog shows 'CRITICAL: Temperature X exceeds critical threshold' immediately before shutdown
show platform temperature (after reboot) shows high temperature watermarks if persisted
thermalctld log contains 'Thermal shutdown' or 'Emergency shutdown initiated'
All fans running at 100% prior to or after restart
causes: Genuine overtemperature: blocked airflow, failed fans, high ambient, overloaded chassis
Sensor miscalibration or stuck sensor reading a falsely high value
Platform plugin returning incorrect temperature values (vendor bug)
Thermal policy thresholds set too low for the operating environment
thermalctld bug causing it to act on stale or invalid sensor data
diagnosis: After reboot: show platform temperature -- check current temps vs thresholds for all sensors
show platform fan -- verify all fans present, spinning, and at reasonable speed (not 100% at idle)
docker exec pmon tail -200 /var/log/pmon/thermalctld.log -- find the sensor name and temperature that triggered shutdown
Check physical: verify no blocked airflow vents, all fan modules seated, ambient temperature in spec
redis-cli -n 6 hgetall 'TEMPERATURE_INFO|<sensor_name>' -- inspect high_threshold and critical_high_threshold values
If one specific sensor: compare its reading to adjacent sensors; outlier = suspect sensor or plugin bug
resolution: Genuine overtemperature: resolve physical cause (clear airflow, replace failed fans, reduce load)
Suspect sensor: 'redis-cli -n 6 hset TEMPERATURE_INFO|<sensor> high_threshold <value>' to temporarily adjust threshold while investigating (NOT a permanent fix)
Platform plugin returning bad data: update sonic-platform-modules BSP package from vendor
If thermal policy too aggressive: vendor platform plugin may expose thermal policy configuration -- consult vendor docs
After fix: monitor 'watch -n 5 show platform temperature' to confirm stability before leaving unattended

common_issues.issue: PSU failure not detected -- no syslog alert for failed PSU
symptoms: show platform psu shows a PSU as absent or failed but no syslog CRIT message was generated
PSU amber LED visible on chassis hardware but SONiC generated no alert
redis-cli -n 6 hgetall 'PSU_INFO|PSU 1' shows empty or stale data
psud log shows no polling activity or daemon is stopped
causes: psud process crashed or was never started (check supervisorctl status)
Platform plugin get_psu() not implemented or raising exception silently
psud polling interval too long relative to alert requirement (default varies by platform)
syslog not configured to forward CRIT-level messages to monitoring system
PSU slot numbering mismatch between platform plugin and physical slot
diagnosis: docker exec pmon supervisorctl status psud -- confirm daemon running
docker exec pmon tail -50 /var/log/pmon/psud.log -- look for 'exception', 'failed', or absence of regular poll log lines
redis-cli -n 6 keys 'PSU_INFO*' -- confirm entries exist (absent = psud not writing)
redis-cli -n 6 hgetall 'PSU_INFO|PSU 1' -- inspect status, presence, voltage, current fields

docker exec pmon python3 -c "import sonic_platform; p = sonic_platform.platform.Platform(); c = p.get_chassis(); psu = c.get_psu(0); print(psu.get_presence(), psu.get_status())" -- test platform API directly
grep -i 'psu\|power' /var/log/syslog | tail -50 -- search for any PSU-related log entries
resolution: psud stopped: 'docker exec pmon supervisorctl start psud' to restart daemon
Platform plugin exception: update BSP package; check vendor support for your hardware revision
Missing PSU_INFO keys: 'docker exec pmon supervisorctl restart psud' -- daemon will repopulate STATE_DB on restart
Confirm fix: 'show platform psu' should show correct presence and status; physically remove and reinsert a PSU to test alert generation
For monitoring: ensure syslog forwarding is configured; psud alerts are syslog CRIT level tagged 'pmon#psud'

common_issues.issue: Fan speed stuck at 100% -- thermalctld fallback mode
symptoms: show platform fan shows all fans at 100% speed regardless of temperature
Audible: fans running at maximum RPM constantly
thermalctld log shows 'Failed to read temperature sensor' or 'Sensor read error'
show platform temperature shows one or more sensors with empty or invalid readings
System is otherwise functional (not shutting down)
causes: One or more temperature sensors returning read errors via platform API
Platform plugin sensor method raising exception that thermalctld catches and treats as fault
Hardware sensor failure (I2C device offline, sensor chip powered down)
thermalctld entered safety fallback: when sensor state is unknown, drive fans to 100% to prevent overtemp
Platform API version mismatch after SONiC upgrade (new thermalctld, old platform plugin)
diagnosis: show platform temperature -- identify which sensor(s) show empty, N/A, or error readings
docker exec pmon tail -100 /var/log/pmon/thermalctld.log -- look for 'sensor read error', 'exception', 'fallback mode'
redis-cli -n 6 keys 'TEMPERATURE_INFO*' -- list all known sensors
redis-cli -n 6 hgetall 'TEMPERATURE_INFO|<suspect_sensor>' -- inspect temperature value field for anomalies

docker exec pmon python3 -c "import sonic_platform; p = sonic_platform.platform.Platform(); c = p.get_chassis(); t = c.get_thermal(0); print(t.get_temperature(), t.get_name())" -- test sensor read directly
dmesg | grep -i 'i2c\|smbus\|thermal' -- check for kernel-level I2C bus errors
resolution: Platform plugin exception: update sonic-platform-modules to latest BSP release for the platform
Hardware sensor fault: identify the failing sensor from thermalctld log, check I2C connectivity ('i2cdetect -y <bus_number>')
API mismatch: ensure sonic-platform-modules version matches SONiC image version (check 'show version' and vendor BSP release notes)
Workaround while investigating: fans at 100% is safe -- do not attempt to manually reduce fan speed while sensor health is unknown
After fix: 'docker exec pmon supervisorctl restart thermalctld' -- daemon re-reads all sensors and exits fallback mode if sensors are healthy

common_issues.issue: Platform API not implemented -- pmon daemon crashes at startup
symptoms: docker exec pmon supervisorctl status shows xcvrd, thermalctld, or psud in FATAL state
daemon log contains 'NotImplementedError' or 'AttributeError' immediately after start
show platform temperature / psu / fan returns empty output or errors
pmon container repeatedly restarting (systemctl status pmon shows high restart count)
causes: Vendor sonic-platform-modules package not installed or wrong version for the hardware
Platform plugin class inheriting from PlatformBase but not overriding required abstract methods
New SONiC feature using a platform API method not present in older vendor plugin
Running SONiC on unsupported or community-ported hardware lacking a complete platform plugin
sonic_platform Python package installed but pointing to wrong platform directory
diagnosis: docker exec pmon supervisorctl status -- identify which specific daemons are in FATAL state
docker exec pmon tail -50 /var/log/pmon/xcvrd.log (or thermalctld.log, psud.log) -- find 'NotImplementedError' traceback
show platform summary -- verify platform name and HWSKU are correctly detected
docker exec pmon python3 -c "import sonic_platform; print(sonic_platform.__file__)" -- verify platform module path
ls /usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/sonic_platform* -- check installed platform package
dpkg -l | grep sonic-platform -- verify platform plugin package name and version
resolution: Install correct BSP: 'sudo apt-get install sonic-platform-<vendor>-<hwsku>' -- obtain package from vendor or SONiC community
After install: 'sudo systemctl restart pmon' -- container will pick up new platform plugin
If vendor plugin incomplete: identify which methods raise NotImplementedError from log traceback; file bug with vendor
Temporary workaround for non-critical daemons (ledd, pcied): disable in /etc/supervisor/conf.d/pmon.conf if causing container restart loop
Confirm fix: 'show platform summary' should show correct platform; 'docker exec pmon supervisorctl status' all daemons should be RUNNING

scale_considerations.transceiver_count: xcvrd scales linearly with port count. On 128-port platforms, initial EEPROM scan at startup takes 30-90 seconds. DOM polling adds per-port I2C overhead. On dense platforms, xcvrd startup delay is normal -- allow 2 minutes before declaring a transceiver missing.
scale_considerations.polling_intervals: thermalctld default polling interval is 60 seconds. psud default is 60 seconds. On chassis with many sensors (>50 thermals), polling may take longer than the interval -- check thermalctld log for 'poll time exceeded interval' warnings.
scale_considerations.i2c_bus_contention: xcvrd, thermalctld, psud, and syseepromd all share the platform I2C bus via the platform API. Simultaneous polling can cause I2C contention on platforms with slow or shared I2C controllers. Vendor platform plugins should serialize access; if not, intermittent read errors may appear under load.
scale_considerations.memory_pressure: pmon daemons are lightweight compared to swss/syncd. Total pmon container RSS is typically under 200MB. Not a concern on production hardware.
